researchMetadata.methodology: Instagram Reels search via Scrape Creators API

keyFindings: Western I-70 corridor (Glenwood Springs/Canyon/Vail) dominates Instagram engagement
Denver metropolitan area shows strong secondary engagement
Mountain peaks (Evans, Bierstadt, Grays) have moderate but consistent content
Highway-specific queries (I-70 X) often outperform location-name-only searches
Niche destinations (Minturn, Battle Mountain) have minimal but devoted creator communities

engagementRankings.contentType: Hot springs, canyons, resort lifestyle, adventure sports
Scenic canyon drives, river views, dramatic geology, highway vistas
Skiing, mountain lifestyle, resort amenities, summer activities, scenic vistas

Mountain town life, shopping, dining, outdoor activities, scenic drives
Lake recreation, mountain activities, scenic overlooks, outdoor sports
High-altitude peak, scenic vistas, wildlife, alpine ecology, climbing
Tunnel engineering, highway driving, scenic mountain transitions
Highway driving experience, scenic transitions, mountain passes
searchVariations: I-70 Vail to Glenwood Colorado - 10 reels
I-70 Vail to Glenwood I-70 - 10 reels

engagementRankings.contentType: Highway driving, mountain transitions, scenic corridors
searchVariations: I-70 Denver to Vail Colorado - 5 reels
I-70 Denver to Vail I-70 - 5 reels

lowTierLocations.note: Niche pass with dedicated outdoor enthusiasts
Small mountain community, limited but consistent content
Scenic pass with strong I-70 driver engagement
Remote pass with outdoor activity focus
Peak content for serious hikers only

Popular fourteener with consistent climbing content

highPriorityStops.rationale: Highest engagement (1.93M views). Official tourism presence. Multiple activity options (hot springs, hiking, river activities). Strong creator infrastructure.
highPriorityStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Hot springs soaking, canyon views, adventure sports, sunset photography

highPriorityStops.rationale: 3rd highest engagement (1.82M views). Established resort infrastructure. June offers summer mountain activities before peak season. Premium dining and accommodations.
highPriorityStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Mountain vistas, hiking trails, town exploration, luxury resort experiences

highPriorityStops.rationale: 2nd highest engagement (1.86M views). Dramatically scenic. Direct I-70 corridor content creator favorite (@i70things, @eu.jeansantos). River activities available.
highPriorityStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Canyon driving, river views, dramatic geology, scenic overlooks

secondaryStops.rationale: 4th highest engagement (1.63M views). Strong food/culture scene in June. Starting point for the drive west on I-70.
secondaryStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Food culture, urban exploration, street art, music venues

secondaryStops.rationale: 5th highest engagement (1.61M views). Convenient I-70 stop with shopping/dining. Lower altitude than surrounding peaks.
secondaryStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Mountain town lifestyle, shopping, casual dining, scenic drive breaks

secondaryStops.rationale: 6th and 14th highest engagement respectively. Lake recreation. Central Colorado location. Water activities in June.
secondaryStops.instagramContentOpportunities: Lake recreation, mountain reflections, scenic drives, outdoor activities

passesAndTunnels.note: 8th highest engagement (1.38M views). Engineering/driving content. Direct I-70 experience.
17th engagement but scenic value. Alternative mountain pass with strong views.

skipRecommendations.location: Minturn, Battle Mountain, Fancy Pass, Red Cliff
skipRecommendations.rationale: Low engagement (37K-361K views each). Remote locations. Limited June activities. Content opportunities minimal compared to main corridor.

skipRecommendations.location: Mt. Bierstadt, Mt. Grays, Mt. Torreys, Mt. Evans, Mt. Sherman
skipRecommendations.rationale: While peaks show content (7-77K avg views), they require significant time commitment. June weather may limit summit attempts. Better content in established towns.
skipRecommendations.instagramValue: Moderate but time-intensive. Only pursue if specifically interested in peak bagging.

topCreatorsByReachAcross25Locations: @i70things - Dedicated I-70 corridor content creator, appears in Glenwood Canyon, I-70 segments, Loveland Pass. HIGH PRIORITY for interviews/collaboration.
@coloradomountaintowns - Regional mountain towns specialist, appears in Vail, Breckenridge, Minturn. Strong local knowledge.
@experience.colorado - Official Colorado tourism, appears in I-70 Denver to Vail segment. Authentic destination content.
9newsdenver - Local news outlet, appears in Dillon, Dillon Reservoir. Community-focused coverage.

methodologyNotes.searchStrategy: Dual keyword approach per location: '[Location] Colorado' + '[Location] I-70' to capture both destination-specific and highway-corridor content
methodologyNotes.resultsCapped: 10 reels per query per API behavior, 20 reels maximum per location
methodologyNotes.engagementMetric: video_view_count or video_play_count from Reels API
dataCollected: Engagement scores (sum of all views per location)
limitations: API returns max ~10 reels per query - may undercount total content
Creator lists show top profiles only, not exhaustive

comparisonToPreviousCalifornia.note: This I-70 research provides direct engagement benchmarks for the Grand American Loop project. June 2026 travel dates can be evaluated against Instagram creator saturation levels.
comparisonToPreviousCalifornia.byComparison: Strong engagement across multiple locations suggests I-70 is well-documented corridor with established content creators and tourism infrastructure
